description: Add unit tests for the new LLM-first conversation pipeline to verify the routing logic works correctly: LLM availability checks, fallback behavior, and RAG context assembly.
steps: Add #include for '../llm/cloud_llm.cpp' and '../llm/llm_engine.cpp' to MK_OS/tests/test_main.cpp (in the includes section around line 75).

Add a new test function test_cloud_llm_initialization() that creates a MKCloudLLM instance and verifies: isAvailable() returns false when no API keys are set (the test environment has no keys), getProviderName() returns 'none' or the first configured provider name.

Add a new test function test_llm_engine_initialization() that creates a MKLLMEngine instance and verifies: isAvailable() returns false when no local server is running (test environment), getServerType() returns 'none'.

Add a new test function test_llm_fallback_logic() that simulates the conversation pipeline logic: when both LLM engines are unavailable (isAvailable() == false), the code should produce an empty string from LLM, causing fallback to Genesis/template system. Verify this by checking that MKCloudLLM.generate() returns empty when not available, and MKLLMEngine.generate() returns empty when not available.

Add a new test function test_rag_context_assembly() that creates a MKPatternGraph, adds a fact (e.g., 'python is_a language'), queries it with getAll('python'), and verifies the results can be formatted as a vector<string> suitable for passing to generateWithContext().
Add a new test function test_brain_memory_context_string() that creates a MKBrainMemory, commits a few turns, and verifies getContextString() returns a properly formatted conversation history string.
Register all new tests with RUN_TEST() in the main() function of test_main.cpp.
Verify with 'cd MK_OS && make test' that all tests (existing and new) pass.
acceptance_criteria: New tests compile as part of 'make test'
test_cloud_llm_initialization passes (verifies unavailable state without API keys)
test_llm_engine_initialization passes (verifies unavailable state without local server)
test_llm_fallback_logic passes (verifies empty returns when unavailable)
All existing tests continue to pass
'cd MK_OS && make test' exits with code 0
verification: Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/MK_os-2/MK_OS && make test' and confirm all tests pass with exit code 0
findings: All 5 new tests pass cleanly. No double-inclusion issues - brain_memory.cpp, cloud_llm.cpp, and llm_engine.cpp all have proper #ifndef guards. The LLM engine constructor triggers a network check (1s timeout) to detect local servers, which adds a small delay to the test but passes fine. Test suite now has 47 tests and 357 assertions, all passing.
